term_inventory_hebrew.meaning:
1) to hurt, pain, grieve, displease, vex, wrest
1a) (Qal) to hurt, pain
1b) (Niphal) to be in pain, be pained, be grieved
1c) (Piel) to vex, torture
1d) (Hiphil) to cause pain
1e) (Hithpael) to feel grieved, be vexed

verse_records.verse_text: And the Lord regretted that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him to his heart.

The sons of Jacob had come in from the field as soon as they heard of it, and the men were indignant and very angry, because he had done an outrageous thing in Israel by lying with Jacob's daughter, for such a thing must not be done.
And now do not be distressed or angry with yourselves because you sold me here, for God sent me before you to preserve life.

But David vowed again, saying, "Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes, and he thinks, 'Do not let Jonathan know this, lest he be grieved.' But truly, as the Lord lives and as your soul lives, there is but a step between me and death."
And Jonathan rose from the table in fierce anger and ate no food the second day of the month, for he was grieved for David, because his father had disgraced him.
So the victory that day was turned into mourning for all the people, for the people heard that day, "The king is grieving for his son."
His father had never at any time displeased him by asking, "Why have you done thus and so?" He was also a very handsome man, and he was born next after Absalom.

Jabez called upon the God of Israel, saying, "Oh that you would bless me and enlarge my border, and that your hand might be with me, and that you would keep me from harm so that it might not bring me pain!" And God granted what he asked.

Then he said to them, "Go your way. Eat the fat and drink sweet wine and send portions to anyone who has nothing ready, for this day is holy to our Lord. And do not be grieved, for the joy of the Lord is your strength."
So the Levites calmed all the people, saying, "Be quiet, for this day is holy; do not be grieved."
All day long they injure my cause; all their thoughts are against me for evil.
How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness and grieved him in the desert!
He who quarries stones is hurt by them, and he who splits logs is endangered by them.
For the Lord has called you like a wife deserted and grieved in spirit, like a wife of youth when she is cast off, says your God.
But they rebelled and grieved his Holy Spirit; therefore he turned to be their enemy, and himself fought against them.
I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent.
The sorrows of those who run after another god shall multiply; their drink offerings of blood I will not pour out or take their names on my lips.
He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
Whoever winks the eye causes trouble, and a babbling fool will come to ruin.
A glad heart makes a cheerful face, but by sorrow of heart the spirit is crushed.

But he said, "My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he is the only one left. If harm should happen to him on the journey that you are to make, you would bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol."
as soon as he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die, and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to Sheol.

as the days on which the Jews got relief from their enemies, and as the month that had been turned for them from sorrow into gladness and from mourning into a holiday; that they should make them days of feasting and gladness, days for sending gifts of food to one another and gifts to the poor.
How long must I take counsel in my soul and have sorrow in my heart all the day? How long shall my enemy be exalted over me?
For my life is spent with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength fails because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
When they are diminished and brought low through oppression, evil, and sorrow,
The snares of death encompassed me; the pangs of Sheol laid hold on me; I suffered distress and anguish.
And the ransomed of the Lord shall return and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads; they shall obtain gladness and joy, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
And the ransomed of the Lord shall return and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads; they shall obtain gladness and joy, and sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
My joy is gone; grief is upon me; my heart is sick within me.
Why did I come out from the womb to see toil and sorrow, and spend my days in shame?
Then shall the young women rejoice in the dance, and the young men and the old shall be merry. I will turn their mourning into joy; I will comfort them, and give them gladness for sorrow.
You said, 'Woe is me! For the Lord has added sorrow to my pain. I am weary with my groaning, and I find no rest.'
you will be filled with drunkenness and sorrow. A cup of horror and desolation, the cup of your sister Samaria;
My soul melts away for sorrow; strengthen me according to your word!
The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son makes a glad father, but a foolish son is a sorrow to his mother.
He who sires a fool gets himself sorrow, and the father of a fool has no joy.
"How long will you torment me and break me in pieces with words?
and I will put it into the hand of your tormentors, who have said to you, 'Bow down, that we may pass over'; and you have made your back like the ground and like the street for them to pass over.
The roads to Zion mourn, for none come to the festival; all her gates are desolate; her priests groan; her virgins have been afflicted, and she herself suffers bitterly.
Her foes have become the head; her enemies prosper, because the Lord has afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions; her children have gone away, captives before the foe.
"Is it nothing to you, all you who pass by? Look and see if there is any sorrow like my sorrow, which was brought upon me, which the Lord inflicted on the day of his fierce anger.
for, though he cause grief, he will have compassion according to the abundance of his steadfast love;
for he does not afflict from his heart or grieve the children of men.
I will gather those of you who mourn for the festival, so that you will no longer suffer reproach.
And they sat with him on the ground seven days and seven nights, and no one spoke a word to him, for they saw that his suffering was very great.
"If I speak, my pain is not assuaged, and if I forbear, how much of it leaves me?
I was mute and silent; I held my peace to no avail, and my distress grew worse.
though you make them grow on the day that you plant them, and make them blossom in the morning that you sow, yet the harvest will flee away in a day of grief and incurable pain.
behold, my servants shall sing for gladness of heart, but you shall cry out for pain of heart and shall wail for breaking of spirit.
Why is my pain unceasing, my wound incurable, refusing to be healed? Will you be to me like a deceitful brook, like waters that fail?
Then the Lord said, "I have surely seen the affliction of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their cry because of their taskmasters. I know their sufferings,
whatever prayer, whatever plea is made by any man or by all your people Israel, each knowing his own affliction and his own sorrow and stretching out his hands toward this house,
"Man is also rebuked with pain on his bed and with continual strife in his bones,
Many are the sorrows of the wicked, but steadfast love surrounds the one who trusts in the Lord.
For I am ready to fall, and my pain is ever before me.
For they persecute him whom you have struck down, and they recount the pain of those you have wounded.
Why do you cry out over your hurt? Your pain is incurable. Because your guilt is great, because your sins are flagrant, I have done these things to you.
Suddenly Babylon has fallen and been broken; wail for her! Take balm for her pain; perhaps she may be healed.
"The Lord is in the right, for I have rebelled against his word; but hear, all you peoples, and see my suffering; my young women and my young men have gone into captivity.
On the third day, when they were sore, two of the sons of Jacob, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers, took their swords and came against the city while it felt secure and killed all the males.
and you shall attack every fortified city and every choice city, and shall fell every good tree and stop up all springs of water and ruin every good piece of land with stones.
For he wounds, but he binds up; he shatters, but his hands heal.
He feels only the pain of his own body, and he mourns only for himself.
But I am afflicted and in pain; let your salvation, O God, set me on high!
Even in laughter the heart may ache, and the end of joy may be grief.
Because you have disheartened the righteous falsely, although I have not grieved him, and you have encouraged the wicked, that he should not turn from his evil way to save his life,
"And for the house of Israel there shall be no more a brier to prick or a thorn to hurt them among all their neighbors who have treated them with contempt. Then they will know that I am the Lord God.
Surely vexation kills the fool, and jealousy slays the simple.
"Oh that my vexation were weighed, and all my calamity laid in the balances!
You renew your witnesses against me and increase your vexation toward me; you bring fresh troops against me.
My eye has grown dim from vexation, and all my members are like a shadow.
"The Lord saw it and spurned them, because of the provocation of his sons and his daughters.
had I not feared provocation by the enemy, lest their adversaries should misunderstand, lest they should say, "Our hand is triumphant, it was not the Lord who did all this."'
And her rival used to provoke her grievously to irritate her, because the Lord had closed her womb.
Do not regard your servant as a worthless woman, for all along I have been speaking out of my great anxiety and vexation.
It was for the sins of Jeroboam that he sinned and that he made Israel to sin, and because of the anger to which he provoked the Lord, the God of Israel.

And I will make your house like the house of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha the son of Ahijah, for the anger to which you have provoked me, and because you have made Israel to sin.
Still the Lord did not turn from the burning of his great wrath, by which his anger was kindled against Judah, because of all the provocations with which Manasseh had provoked him.
My eye wastes away because of grief; it grows weak because of all my foes.
But you do see, for you note mischief and vexation, that you may take it into your hands; to you the helpless commits himself; you have been the helper of the fatherless.
Be gracious to me, O Lord, for I am in distress; my eye is wasted from grief; my soul and my body also.
Restore us again, O God of our salvation, and put away your indignation toward us!
The vexation of a fool is known at once, but the prudent ignores an insult.
A foolish son is a grief to his father and bitterness to her who bore him.
It is better to live in a desert land than with a quarrelsome and fretful woman.
A stone is heavy, and sand is weighty, but a fool's provocation is heavier than both.
For in much wisdom is much vexation, and he who increases knowledge increases sorrow.
For all his days are full of sorrow, and his work is a vexation. Even in the night his heart does not rest. This also is vanity.
Moreover, all his days he eats in darkness in much vexation and sickness and anger.
Sorrow is better than laughter, for by sadness of face the heart is made glad.
Be not quick in your spirit to become angry, for anger lodges in the heart of fools.
Remove vexation from your heart, and put away pain from your body, for youth and the dawn of life are vanity.

For when I had brought them into the land that I swore to give them, then wherever they saw any high hill or any leafy tree, there they offered their sacrifices and there they presented the provocation of their offering; there they sent up their pleasing aromas, and there they poured out their drink offerings.

cross_registry_links.note: ka.a.s (H3708B) and ka.as (H3708A) both carry primary meanings of anger and provocation alongside grief, placing them at the intersection of the grief and anger word sets. Verses such as 1Ki 15:30 and 2Ki 23:26 concern divine anger provoked by Israel.
a.tsav (H6087A) appears in anger-proximate contexts (Gen 34:7 — indignation; 1Sa 20:34 — fierce anger) and its meaning range includes displease and vex, overlapping with anger terminology.
ya.gon (H3015) is defined as grief, sorrow, anguish, creating direct semantic overlap with any anguish word set. Psa 116:3 records both distress and anguish in the same verse.
Jer 20:18 pairs sorrow (ya.gon) with shame in a single lament, connecting the grief and shame word sets.
Isa 63:10 uses a.tsav to describe Israel grieving the Holy Spirit — a theologically significant connection relevant to the Holy Spirit project.

data_quality_flags.description: ka.a.s (H3708B) and ka.as (H3708A) share identical occurrence counts (26 x), near-identical related words clusters, and STEP notes that H3708A 'also means' H3708B. They appear to be spelling or form variants of the same lemma. They are retained as separate entries per the source file but flagged for review in later analysis phases.
See ka.a.s (H3708B) note above. The two entries for vexation share meaning range, occurrence count, and related words, and the source explicitly cross-references them.

ka.a.s (H3708B) has a stated occurrence count of approximately 26. The source file records 4 verses, approximately 15% of the occurrence count — below the 20% threshold. Register assessments may not be fully representative.

makh.ov (H4341) had no word analysis block in the original source file (Check B Hard Stop in Stage 1). Resolved prior to extraction: user supplied a complete word analysis block including Strong's confirmation, occurrence count, and full meaning. Verse records were already present.

tu.gah (H8424) has only 3 verse records against a stated occurrence count of approximately 4, giving approximately 75% coverage. Sample is adequate but the full occurrence set is very small. Meaning range assessment is limited.
ya.gon (H3015) records 14 verses against a stated occurrence count of approximately 14 — effectively complete coverage of the occurrence set.
